observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs. b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required. c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit. d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the BRE; accounts, appearing in the LGU financial statements.
observations.reason: Insufficient man power to be delegated for the composition of inventory and disposal committee

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prepare the CY2020 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities Undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the prescribed period.

We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-mentioned reports within the Prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Management shall
a) Make a representation with the authorized depository banks to provide
copies of bank statements. And if the bank cannot immediately provide the
bank statements, electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions for each
bank account is sufficient to enable the accounting unit to update the
preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements; and

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare prompily the monthly BRS within
ten (10) days afier the receipt of Bank Statement.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory
count annually; and

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed PPELC; and

c. Instruct the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit a copy to the Audit Team for
evaluation.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Management:

a) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RIS to support every issuance of
supplies and inventories to requisitioning units or end-users; prepare
and submit the SSMI to the Accounting Unit as basis for the recording of
related expenses;

b) The Municipal Accountant, Municipal GSO and/or Municipal Treasurer
shall prepare and maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock
Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of their records; and
update the same based on the resulls of the physical inventory; and

¢) The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least
semi-annually, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year.

Recommendations:

We recommend that the Management:

a) Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for all accounts, as well as the
corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). Ensure also that the account
titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same
with that of the SL and the Financial Statements; and

b) Maintain all the necessary journals, regisiries, ledgers and other relevant
supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting
standards

Recommendation:

We recommend the Management that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal
Engineer must submit the list of all on — going government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year
and submit monthly monitoring PPA’s report of the agency, as stated in COA
Circular 2013 — 004.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal GSO shall prepare and submit to the Audit
Team the monthly report of fuel consumption and the Report of fuel consumption
per vehicle as required

Recommendation:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to instruct concerned accountable officers
to timely submit the necessary reports and supporting documents fo
substantiate the reliability and accuracy of the amounts presented in
financial statements and to facilitate the timely submission o the Office of
the Auditor; and

b) Ensure that the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents
of the ensuing years are within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:

a) Adhere and comply with RA 9003 and create a Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board;

b) Prepare compliant Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan;

¢) Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid
waste; and

d) Submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan
and the Executive Order evidencing the creation of Municipal Solid
‘Waste Management Board.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to all Revenue Collectors prepare and
submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the
CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable
Jforms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs
and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the
COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer
(LDRRMO) shall:

a) Prepare and submit the required annual LDRRMFIP and to review the
accuracy of the appropriation for pre and post disaster programs,
projects and activities and particularly for the Quick Response Fund:
and

b) Prepare and submit the Report of Utilization Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund within 15 days after the end of each month, using
Annex J-1 format, as provided for under the said COA Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Management to comply with COA Circular 2014 and submit
a copy of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) CY2021 and the GAD
Accomplishment to the Audit Team.
We further recommend that for ensuing years the Management shall, within the
prescribed period given under the rulcs, preparc aud submit the nocessary report
pertaining to GAD.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2021 and submit to the Audit Team. We
Jurther recommend that for the ensuing years, the SCBAA should be submitted
immediately to the Audit Team for review.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC, likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of the approved BGCM budget and non-submission of its revised Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for BGCM PPA’s, precluded the Auditor from conducting timely audit of the BGCM’s transactions in the Agency’s books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of all the PPE. We further recommend that the management should prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as required in Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on NGAs for LGUs.

We recommend that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We recommended that the Management: Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed. b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given) Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements. c. Make sure also that the beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the GL and SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous years. d. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial Statements. e. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and
verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items
requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the
financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank
accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property
management for LGUs.
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required.
c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always
reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should
be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts
appearing in the LGU financial statements.

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend
further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160,
RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget
and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary
requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2020 GAD
Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and
activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the
GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that
GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the
prescribed period.

We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-
mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of
transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA. We further recommend
that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for
review.

(1) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 of the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (2) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and (3) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the Commission. Monthly: 1. Report on Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Utilization of Funds – COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 Quarterly: 1. Report on Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) – COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 2. Gender and Development Utilization – COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 Semestral Reports: 1. Unliquidated Cash Advances – COA Memo No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017 2. Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses – Office of the Auditor Annually: 1. Gender and Development Accomplishment Report - COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 2. Report on Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Utilization of Funds – COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.
b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given) Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.
c. Make sure also that the beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the GL and SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous years.
d. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial Statements.
e. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/reports required by established accounting standards

Recommendation:
We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis for auditor’s immediate
review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts
in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for
all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommend the Management to:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property
management for LGUs.
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required.
c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always
reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which
should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the
PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.
e. The Accounting Office should maintain subsidiary records for the Water
Supply System and Hospitals and Health Center and reconcile the balances
and make the necessary adjustments in the account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the
documentary requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds
transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item
3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were
utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendations:
We reiterate our recommendation for the Management to:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and
Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to
Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office
of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily
basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial
statements.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Management:
a. Shall conduct a regular monitoring of all on-going projects, programs
and activities and prepare monthly accomplishment reports.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Quarterly, Semestral and
Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed
period set by the Commission.

Recommendations:
We reiterate our recommendation that the Management:
a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting
from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the
transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary
and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of
financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the
established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately
correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.
b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given)
Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary
Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure
also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the
GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.
c. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial
Statements.
d. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant
supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

(1) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and (2) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and (3) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

Annually:

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of this Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445,

We _recommend that the  Audit Team with the copies Agreement  (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies No. 2012 to  evaluate whether the funds  were utilized within the specified purpose.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from January-November, 2012 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

We further recommended that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of this Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the personnel assigned to prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

Observe properly COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 that prescribed the deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, and reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.
